step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("percentage SSI rates in clean-contaminated surgical site infections")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the retrieved evidence, we find that the percentage of SSI rates in patients with clean-contaminated wounds ranges widely, generally falling somewhere between 0.5% to 3%. Given the variability noted in different studies and contexts, option B (<10%) appears to encompass this range adequately. Although some references suggest higher rates nearing 10%, the majority indicate values much lower, fitting better within our established bounds. Thus, I concur with Option B (\boxed{B}) as representing a conservative upper limit for expected SSI occurrences in clean-contaminated surgical sites. Discrepancies seen elsewhere reflect diverse methodologies and populations studied, highlighting the necessity of context-specific evaluations when applying generalized statistics. Nonetheless, staying below 10% remains a realistic expectation for many scenarios involving these types of wounds.
